title: brain git approve <runId> [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: Privileged fast-forward-only integration of a review-pending run into canonical. The broker challenge is bound to the exact signed agent commit; on authorization, performs a fast-forward-only compare-and-swap of that commit onto canonical head, then reindexes. If the run's base is stale relative to current canonical head, the CAS refuses with the stable code "refresh-required" (exit 6) — the caller must `git refresh` then re-approve. Authorized ONLY by an OS-presence assertion bound to the broker challenge (op git approve) or the non-interactive --export-challenge → sign → --authorization flow (security-broker-contract.md §7); --yes never authorizes. Idempotent: re-approving an already-integrated run returns the existing integrated commit. Without an authorization, exits 6 (action-required) and, with --export-challenge, emits an AuthorizationChallenge (security-broker-contract.md §7.1) instead of this success object.

integratedCommit.description: 40-char hex commit that was fast-forward integrated (the exact signed commit).

canonicalHead.description: 40-char hex canonical head after integration (equals integratedCommit on a clean FF).

reindexed.description: Mandatory completion postcondition: an exit-0 approve success ALWAYS reflects a completed post-integration reindex. A non-reindexed / in-progress outcome is not this success object.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Broker-authorized, challenge-bound FF-only CAS of the exact signed commit onto canonical, then reindex; stale base ⇒ refresh-required (exit 6); idempotent.

args.description: The review-pending run to approve and integrate.

flags.description: Emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge (op git approve, bound to the exact signed agent commit) to stdout and exit 6 instead of integrating; sign it out of band with an enrolled approver key.
Path to a signed AuthorizationResponse produced from a prior --export-challenge; presents it to the broker to authorize the integration.

Caller-supplied idempotency key; a repeat approve with the same key + request-hash (requestHashScope) returns the existing integrated commit rather than re-integrating (registry idempotency: key-accepting).

sideEffects: fast-forward-only CAS of the signed commit onto canonical head
prohibitedEffects: never a non-fast-forward merge or rewrite of canonical history
never integrates a commit other than the exact challenge-bound signed commit
never authorized by --yes; never bypasses the broker challenge
never runs without the canonical-integration exclusive lock

errorCodes.when: unknown flag/argument, missing <runId>, or --export-challenge combined with --authorization
no --authorization supplied (challenge exported with --export-challenge)
the run's base is stale relative to current canonical head; refresh then re-approve (stable code; surfaces broker authz.canonical_moved)
broker drift: canonicalBaseCommit is no longer the canonical tip (security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker drift: targetCommit/runId differs from the re-derived challenge
broker: the challenge nonce expired (expiresAt passed)
broker: the OS presence assertion is absent or not bound to the challenge
broker: Ed25519 signature verification failed
broker: signingPayload differs from the broker-recomputed canonical bytes
broker: the challenge/response fails the contracts schema (universal broker validation failure, security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: the response's payloadCanonicalization id is unknown (universal broker validation failure, security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: signerId is not in the signer registry
broker: the signer is revoked
broker: the signer is not permitted for this op
broker: the nonce was not issued by this broker
broker: the nonce was already consumed
<runId> does not exist or is not approvable
another integration holder is active
the fail-closed backup watermark blocks a ledger-writing run
unexpected internal failure during integration
